explanation: The command uses the -O flag (equivalent to --output) and omits the local filename, which is allowed by curl. The URL is enclosed in single quotes to avoid issues with special characters. This maintains the same behavior as the original command while using substitution and insertion techniques.

The command replaces --output with --remote-name, which is functionally equivalent. No quotes are used since the URL does not contain spaces or special characters that require escaping. This is a valid substitution technique.

The command uses the short form of --output (-o) and specifies the output filename explicitly. This is a valid substitution and reordering technique that maintains the same behavior as the original command.
The command uses the --output flag with an equals sign (=) to specify the output filename. This is a valid syntax variation of the original command and is functionally equivalent.
This is the original command with no changes. It is included to confirm that the baseline behavior is captured and that the rule will detect it as a true positive.
